case_title: MAC MANAGEMENT LLC VS. ANATOLI TAUSTYKA ET AL

docket_entries.description: CIVIL CASE COVERSHEET FILED (TRANSACTION ID # 74284927) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MAC MANAGEMENT LLC
SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 74357566), PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF MAC MANAGEMENT LLC SERVED SEP-14-2024, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT TAUSTYKA, SIARHEI

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND RELATED CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSPIRACY WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FRAUD AGAINST EACH DEFENDANT WITH REQUISITE SPECIFICITY AS TO EACH ELEMENT, INCLUDING DAMAGES. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE A CONTROVERSY FOR WHICH NO REMEDY AT LAW IS AVAILABLE AND WHICH MAY BE ADDRESSED BY A DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. CASES CITED BY PLAINTIFF IN OPPOSITION FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT DECLARATORY RELIEF IS A PROPER CAUSE OF ACTION DO NOT SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT. IN OROZCO V. WPV SAN JOSE, LLC (2019) 36 CAL.APP.5TH 375 "AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, THE TRIAL COURT RULED IT WAS "IMPOSSIBLE AND UNNECESSARY" TO ISSUE THE REQUESTED RELIEF AND DECLINED TO DO SO" AND THIS RULING WAS NOT THE SUBJECT OF APPEAL. PAVLOVICH V. NEIDHARDT (1954) 128 CAL.APP.2D 559 DID NOT INVOLVE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF APPEARS TO ARGUE ENTITLED TO AN ELECTION TO RESCIND THE CONTRACT AND SEEK RESTORATION TO THEIR ORIGINAL POSITION, PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE AN APPROPRIATE CAUSE OF ACTION IN LIEU OF THE DECLARATORY RELIEF. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Joanna Kozubal Esq., 415-864-6962, via zoom for plaintiff
Aleksandr Volkov Esq., Volkov Law Firm, Inc., 415-987-7000 via zoom for defendant

Ms. Kozubal contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as to the causes of action for fraud and related cause of action for conspiracy with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege fraud against each defendant with requisite specificity as to each element, including damages. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a controversy for which no remedy at law is available and which may be addressed by a declaratory relief cause of action. Cases cited by Plaintiff in opposition for the proposition that declaratory relief is a proper cause of action do not support Plaintiff's argument. In Orozco v. WPV San Jose, LLC (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 375
"As to the cause of action for declaratory relief, the trial court ruled it was "impossible and unnecessary" to issue the requested relief and declined to do so" and this ruling was not the subject of appeal. Pavlovich v. Neidhardt (1954) 128 Cal.App.2d 559 did not involve a cause of action for declaratory relief. To the extent Plaintiff appears to argue entitled to an election to rescind the contract and seek restoration to their original position, Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege an appropriate cause of action in lieu of the declaratory relief.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
